description.content: A collection of API operations related to devices.

item.name:
Gets all of the grows devices, including latest data.
Sparklines (data for the last hour) are not included

request.name:
Gets all of the grows devices, including latest data.
Sparklines (data for the last hour) are not included

response.name: OK

description.content: A collection of API operations related to devices.
Gets the last data point for a device

variable.description: (Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve the latest data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve the latest data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve the latest data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve the latest data.

item.name: Data Range for Device. Each Datapoint in range will be counted towards your api usage limit.

request.name: Data Range for Device. Each Datapoint in range will be counted towards your api usage limit.

description.content: Retrieves all datapoints within a specified timespan the device.

query.description: (Required) The beginning time of your data range in ISO 8601 format.
The end time of your data range in ISO 8601 format. If not provided, API will use the current time.

variable.description: (Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve data.
(Required) The device identifier for which to retrieve data.

description.content: Retrieves all of the device ids assigned to the grow.
Retrieves detailed information for all devices on the grow.

response.name: OK

item.name: Retrieves (one page worth of) light readings(including spectrum) for a given device, supports pagination.

request.name: Retrieves (one page worth of) light readings(including spectrum) for a given device, supports pagination.

query.description: Object containing requested page position. Note that page numeration starts from zero

item.name:
Remotely triggers a light reading off of a Pulse Pro.
The Result needs to be fetched separately in 5-10 seconds
There is a small chance of the Pro failing to trigger a light reading remotely
If no new data is found - retrigger in 1 minute

description.content: A collection of API operations related to users.

item.name: List of users and their data for api key

request.name: List of users and their data for api key

description.content: Retrieves all users for the grow that is bound the the api key.

# Introduction

The Pulse API is an HTTP API that is designed to interact with your pulse account and devices. 

Note that not all app functionality has yet been exposed through this api, so if you need additional functionality, contact our support team at support@pulsegrow.com and we will add it for you. 

This API reference includes all technical documentation developers need to integrate third-party applications and platforms. If you have any additional questions send an email to our support team at support@pulsegrow.com

# Authentication

All HTTP requests made against the Pulse API must be validated with an API Key.

### Acquiring an API Key

To generate a new api key go to https://app.pulsegrow.com/account, scroll down to the api key section, and press the "Add Api Key" button.

### Using Your API Key 

Note: API keys are generated per grow and only have access to that grow's resources (users, devices, etc). 

All requests should target domain `https://api.pulsegrow.com`.

You need to provide the API key via the `x-api-key` header.

Security Warning: It's important to secure your API Key against public access. Guard and secure it like a password. If you suspect that your API key has been compromised, revoke it via the web application at https://app.pulsegrow.com/account. 

Sample request:

```

curl --request GET "https://api.pulsegrow.com/devices/{deviceId}/recent-data" --header "x-api-key: {apiKey}"

``` 

### Api consumption

 Api consumption is measured by number of datapoints retrieved.

 An api call that does not retrieve datapoints counts as one datapoint.

 Hobbyist users can use up to 4800 datapoints daily, Enthusiasts and Professionals 24000 and 120000, respectively 



Contact Support:
 Name: Need help? Contact support
 Email: support@pulsegrow.com
